plans.title: 4-Week Scanning Foundation
plans.description: Build the scanning habit from the ground up with progressive challenges. Perfect for players new to systematic scanning training.

sessions.notes: Focus on getting comfortable with the vibrations. Take your time scanning a full 180° after each signal.
Notice if you start anticipating the vibration. That is normal — we will break that pattern later.
Increase to 20 minutes. Combine with ball work — wall passes or technical drills work well.
Faster intervals — you now have half the time to scan. Focus on efficient head movement.
Combine with movement. Scan while jogging lightly — as it happens in a match.
Active recovery — back to Beginner but longer session. Consolidate what you have learned.
Use the Premium pattern Pre-Match Warmup. Progressively faster — perfect as a warm-up before training.
Decision Maker uses irregular intervals — you cannot predict the next vibration. Your brain must stay alert.
High intensity. Short intervals test your scanning automation. Rest afterwards.
Match Tempo simulates match rhythm with varying pressure. You are ready for this now.
Longer session with Decision Maker. Goal: scanning should be starting to feel automatic.
Final session — back to Intermediate to measure progress. Does it feel easier than week 2?

plans.title: Pre-Season Intensive
plans.description: 2-week high-intensity program to maximise scanning frequency before the season starts. For players with basic scanning experience.

sessions.notes: Start with irregular intervals to reactivate your scanning reflexes after a break.
Match-realistic pattern. Combine with ball work throughout the entire session.
High intensity midweek. Focus on quality over quantity.
Longer session with moderate intervals — active recovery from Wednesday's intensity.
End the week with match focus. Set a goal: scan 360° at every single signal.
Increase duration — you should now reach near-match scanning fatigue.
Position-specific session. Focus on back-space scanning and pressure awareness.
Maximum intensity midweek. Scanning should now feel nearly automatic.
Use the warmup pattern to prepare for tomorrow's match or test.
Season opener. Test yourself: are you scanning now without thinking about it?

plans.title: Weekly Maintenance Program
plans.description: For players who have completed the foundation course. Maintain and develop scanning automation throughout the season.

sessions.notes: Irregular intervals keep scanning automation sharp. Always combine with ball work or movement.
Match-realistic pattern. Visualise match situations during training to strengthen the mental connection.
